name: Pennsylvania Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.streetAddress:
One Penn Center
2601 N. 3rd Street
Harrisburg, PA 17110
address.description:
State Board of Medicine
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the State Board of Nursing.

requiredDocuments.name: Examination Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Verification of a passing score on the CDR or BCNS exam, sent directly from the testing agency.

requiredDocuments.name: Child Abuse CE Certificate
requiredDocuments.description: A certificate proving completion of the mandatory 3-hour Pennsylvania child abuse recognition and reporting course.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to get an LDN license in Pennsylvania?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee for a Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionist in Pennsylvania is $95. After issuance, it costs $71 to renew the license biennially.

mainEntity.name: Which Pennsylvania board issues the dietitian license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unlike many states with dedicated dietetics boards, Pennsylvania regulates Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionists under the State Board of Nursing within the broader Department of State.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Pennsylvania dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: To renew biennially, you must complete 30 hours of continuing professional education. Of those 30 hours, 2 hours must strictly be a Board-approved course in child abuse recognition and reporting.

mainEntity.name: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Pennsylvania?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
